root_cause_candidates.cause: Resource contention / latency bottleneck in ts-auth-service leading to severe tail latency (p99Δms +506), causing downstream ts-preserve-service to experience propagated delays and errors.
root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All sources converge on ts-auth-service as the upstream bottleneck causing downstream symptom in ts-preserve-service. Log Agent highlights ts-auth-service with significant latency and downstream impact; Topology confirms a plausible propagation path from ts-auth-service to ts-preserve-service. The TCB-RCA baseline did not identify ts-auth-service as a root candidate (it prioritized ts-preserve-service), but given the strong timing/latency evidence and structural plausibility, ts-auth-service is more credible as the root cause. The absence of explicit error logs in ts-auth-service is consistent with a latency/resource contention fault that may not generate logs but does impact downstream calls.

final_verdict.cause: Resource contention / latency bottleneck in ts-auth-service leading to severe tail latency (p99Δms +506), causing downstream ts-preserve-service to experience propagated delays and errors.

notes: Candidate 'ts-auth-service' supported by 1 modality (has_log_evidence); confidence capped at 0.6.

agent_results.summary: Incident at ts-preserve-service: 'Service ts-auth-service experiencing socket fault'. No error logs found. LLM hypothesis:  ts-auth-service exhibits severe tail latency (p99Δms +506) indicating a latency or processing bottleneck; ts-preserve-service downstream relies on ts-auth-service and shows issues as a result. ts-price-service and ts-food-map-service also show strong metric anomalies (cpu_z > 30) suggesting upstream contention or degraded capacity at authentication/authorization paths propagating to downstream services.

metadata.hypothesis: ts-auth-service exhibits severe tail latency (p99Δms +506) indicating a latency or processing bottleneck; ts-preserve-service downstream relies on ts-auth-service and shows issues as a result. ts-price-service and ts-food-map-service also show strong metric anomalies (cpu_z > 30) suggesting upstream contention or degraded capacity at authentication/authorization paths propagating to downstream services.
metadata.llm_reasoning: Metric signals identify ts-auth-service as having the strongest anomaly (p99Δms +506 >100). ts-price-service and ts-food-map-service also show CPU spikes (>30) indicating resource contention. Given ts-preserve-service depends on multiple downstream services including ts-auth-service, the upstream latency/resource pressure on ts-auth-service is likely propagating failures/latency to the preserve path.
